title: oxalis hirsutibulba
intro: Oxalis hirsutibulba, also known as the hairy-leaved wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Oxalis hirsutibulba is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to support kidney function and act as a diuretic. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting detoxification. Oxalis hirsutibulba can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and roots can be used in herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
